description:
Dark Elegance: The Exhibition - Opening Night, Dark Elegance.
The subtle dance between grace, melancholy, and beauty - that can only be attained when we peer into the depths of our shadows.
This event is the opening night. The exhibition will be available for viewing until Nov. 29th from Tuesday to Saturday, 10am to 6pm.

description:
Wai Art Show, Enjoy the Wairarapa’s largest cash & carry art show event!

The Wai Art Show exhibits a diverse and exquisite range of 2D and 3D artworks by local artists and from across New Zealand. The 3-day event attracts over 3,000 visitors and coincides with the Carterton Daffodil Day – an event not to be missed by art lovers!

description:
Kapiti Kirtan, Kapiti Kirtan
Come and join us in an evening of mantra, music, and connection.
We’ll gather in community, lift our voices together, and let the vibrations carry us to stillness and joy.

No experience needed — just an open heart and the willingness to sing.

Bring a friend, bring your voice, bring your presence.
Let’s chant, breathe, and celebrate life together.
All welcome. Koha
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Book Launch: Bird of the Year, We're so excited to celebrate our feathery friends at the book launch of Bird of the Year, published by Penguin.
Each year, thousands of us take part in Forest & Bird's campaign to bring awareness to the challenges these feathery friends face and to see who comes out on top as the coveted Bird of the Year.
We're grateful to Forest & Bird for their support of this event. We hope to see you there!

description: Laughs on Draughts - Comedy Night, Flash of Jeanius Production and Baylands Brewery present Laughs on Draughts - Comedy Night! Join us for an evening of top-notch stand-up comedy featuring hilarious comedians and, of course, your favorite craft beers. Whether you’re here for the punchlines or the pints (or both), this is the perfect way to enjoy your night. Grab your crew, sip something delicious, and let the good vibes flow. Cheers to jokes and hops!

description:
National Poetry Day: It's All Dimples This City - Poems of Wellington at Unity, Join us in the shop to hear from six excellent poets; Cadence Chung, Michael Fitzsimons, Fiona Kidman, Arihia Latham, Mary McCallum and Jackson McCarthy. They will share their own poetry that speaks to this wind-blown harbour city and also share a poem from a local poet that they believe also captures the heart of Te Whanganui-a-Tara.
Happy National Poetry Day! See you there!
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Instant Theatre, Instant Theatre is a fully improvised, character-driven performance of real people in real situations—created spontaneously before your eyes.

With no scripts, no games, and nothing prepared, our four performers build a rich, interwoven world exploring the lives of these characters experiencing joy, heartbreak, unexpected life events, human connection, revelations of who they truly are.

Every show is a one-night-only window into these lives.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Everything But The Medicine. Wellington. Performance and book launch., GP Lucy O'Hagan launches her book Everything But the Medicine,
Lucy will perform 'Inside the GP consultation' and read from her book.
'Stunning. kind, joyful and wise. Everything but the Medicine...is very much a medicine itself.' Glenn Colquhoun
Book sales and signings.
Bar and nibbles from 6pm.
Supported by the wonderful Unity Books, Wellington
Big thanks to publisher Massey University Press.

Hosted by Always Becominging and including readings by: Nick Ascroft, Hinemoana Baker, Kate Camp, Leah Dodd, Sylvan Spring and Stacey Teague.
Friday 22 August, 6pm
The Undercurrent, 118 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington
Free event, all welcome
Come through or the FOMO will come for you!
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Writers on Mondays: Short Sharp Script 2, Circa Theatre
Another fast, funny, and furious set of scripts from the IIML’s scriptwriting students. This week the spotlight falls on work by Iris Mackenzie, Hazel Perigo-Blackburn, Megan Connolly, Sonja Cooke, and Victoria Lewis, introduced by Ken Duncum.

ADMISSION IS FREE—ALL WELCOME
Writers on Mondays is presented with the Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, Circa Theatre, Meow and Phantom Billstickers National Poetry Day and supported by the Letteri Family.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Exhibition Opening: Echoes of the Wild by Rose Dryland, Inverlochy Art School warmly welcomes you to the opening celebration of Rose Dryland's first solo show "Echoes of the Wild".
Join us to celebrate with the artist and take in her impressive collection of works shaped by time spent outdoors "in the quiet company of trees, wind, and weather".
Moving between sky, water, forest, and land, they offer a chance to slow down, to breathe, and to notice the wild beauty that is always present, though often overlooked.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Writers on Mondays: Short Sharp Script 1, Circa theatre
Actors perform dynamic new work by ma scriptwriting students from the iiml in this entertaining hour of theatre. This week, scripts by alex cherian, chantal euringer, crosby allen-jennings, eva brunel, and leroy nurkka are introduced by ken duncum.

Admission is free—all welcome
Writers on Mondays is presented with the Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, Circa Theatre, Meow and Phantom Billstickers National Poetry Day and supported by the Letteri Family.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Noye's Fludde, Wellington Cathedral of St Paul proudly presents a vibrant community production of Benjamin Britten’s beloved opera, Noye’s Fludde — a retelling of the biblical story of Noah’s Ark.
Composed especially for performers of all ages and abilities, this imaginative work features:
A charming chorus of masked animals
A spirited orchestra of young musicians performing alongside members of the New Zealand Symphony Orchestra
With stunning visuals and uplifting music, this is a performance the whole family will enjoy!
label: Arts & Theatre

description: St. Theresa's School Production 2025 - LEST WE FORGET, Set in both New Zealand and the narrow trenches of Gallipoli, Lest We Forget tells the powerful story of New Zealand civilians, soldiers and nurses who find courage, friendship and hope in the face of war. As letters from home arrive and battles rage on, they share laughter, sorrow, and dreams of returning to Aotearoa. This school production honours the Kiwi spirit and sacrifices made during one of our nation’s most defining campaigns. A moving tribute not to be missed.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
There once was a boy..., Written and performed by Robert Lloyd.
A boy is stranded at sea. A storm bears down on him, and, in what he believes are his final moments, he ponders his life and the choice he made to come here—to explore what’s over the curious horizon—and wonders: Was this a mistake? There once was a boy… is a fun, poetic performance for tweens, teens and their whānau. This live “pop-up book” explores curiosity, courage, and finding light in the darkness.
Recommended for audiences aged 10+. Contains flashing lights.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Springlands School Gala 2025, Springlands School Gala 2025
Join us at the Springlands School Gala for a fun-filled family day out.

Enjoy bouncy castles, a chocolate wheel, face painting, bake sale, food stalls, plant sale, silent auction, kid's disco, secondhand toys and books, and much, much more!

💥 Saturday 13 September
🎪 Springlands School, 20 Murphys Road, Blenheim
⏰ 10am-2pm, rain or shine.

We look forward to seeing you there!

Also check out other Parties in Blenheim, Arts events in Blenheim, Fine Arts events in Blenheim.
label: Arts & Theatre

description: SGCNZ National Shakespeare School Production 2025, Come and see the culmination of an exceptional week's work by the 46 rangatahi selected from SGCNZ VUW's Regional and National Shakespeare Festivals - plus a costume and music competition winner - in 40 minutes performances from As You Like It, The Tempest and Julius Caesar directed by David O'Donnell, Jade Eriksen and Jacqueline Coats. Enjoy the crème dela crème from throughout the motu, with their multi-faceted talents in the play excerpts and singing workshop. Accessible for all!
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Writers on Mondays: The Next Page 2, 6–7.30 pm at Meow
The next new wave of words comes in with Simon Sweetman, Vicci Ho, Will Salmon, Ken Arkind, Te Ariki Wi Neera, Niamh Vaughan, Zoë Meager, Tasmin Pritchard, Stephenjohn Holgate, Margaret Mitchell, Isabella Smith, Helena Leon Mayer, Alexandra Stronach, Danielle Heyhoe, and Mitch Marks.

ADMISSION IS FREE—ALL WELCOME

label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Kevin Bloody Wilson WELLINGTON NZ, It’s been more than 40 years since Kevin Bloody Wilson dropped his very first album – the one that went quadruple platinum and cemented him as a musical comedy icon!
Fast forward 20+ albums later, and this Aussie legend’s international fan base just keeps growing, proving that Kev’s no-BS, PC-free style is here to stay.
From TikTok to YouTube, or his legendary live shows, Kev's unmatched DILLIGAF attitude is inspiring new generations to laugh, sing, and is a welcome reminder to not take life too seriously. He’s more than a storyteller – he’s a way of life.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Raw Meat Monday, Raw Meat Monday is back for 2025! Wellington's longest-running open mic comedy night.

Comedy gold and comedy coal, each night is a guaranteed gamble. If you’re trying comedy for the first time, Raw Meat is the safest place to start. Featuring a guest MC and a changing lineup of first-time acts, seasoned pros, and everything in between.

Interested in performing? Email the Humorous Arts Trust at contact@humorous.co.nz to book a spot.

Please be advised, the show may contain adult themes and strong language

Thanks to Cameron Harrison Butchery and Delicatessen for sponsoring the raffle!
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Iron, Iron
By Rona Munro
Directed by Campbell Wright
27th August - 8th September
The Gryphon Theatre
Iron follows the rekindling of a relationship between Josie and her estranged mother Fay who is currently serving a life sentence for killing Josie's father. Josie seeks to understand the events that she barely has any memory of, while Fay attempts to live life vicariously through her. As the play develops, we discover how incarceration has affected all of the parties involved, including the prison guards.
The Cast
Faye - Karen Anslow
Josie - Ivana Palezevic
Guard 1 - James Bayliss
Guard 2 - Helen Mackenzie-Hughes
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Behind the Seams, Behind the Seams
In celebration of WOW’s 20th anniversary in Wellington, meet our costume artisans and discover how legendary characters leap from page to screen – stitch by stitch.

At our exclusive events, we invite you to step into the creative heart of Wētā Workshop and uncover the magic woven into the world's most extraordinary cinematic costumes.

Meet the makers | Craft unveiled | Witness the magic in action

Costume enthusiasts, creatives and WOW fans—this is your exclusive behind-the-scenes pass.

Don't miss the magic and reserve your spot today!

Also check out other Workshops in Wellington.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Catfish Trifecta presents: Improv on a plate, Welcome to Improv on a Plate, the comedy tasting menu no one ordered and everyone regrets.
In this unholy fusion of high-stakes improv and low-stakes judgment, three deranged guest chefs (aka format-pitchers) take the stage with one mission: serve up the most unhinged, impossible improv formats they can dream of. Enter Wellington comedy trio, Catfish Trifecta — your unsuspecting improv victims — who must swallow each cursed format whole and perform it on the spot.
No script. No escape. Just raw, unfiltered chaos served à la mode.
Tickets $10
Door sales while available.
Venue R18.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Meet Your Candidates, Meet Your Candidates
Porirua’s local elections are in October 2025 – come and meet the candidates who want to represent you as mayor, and as councillors in the Parirua Māori and Onepoto Wards.

Join us for a friendly, panel-style event where you’ll hear about the candidates’ backgrounds, their connections to the Bay, and share a few laughs too.

There will be no questions taken from the floor on the night but you are welcome to send your questions before hand to

Let’s talk about the future of our community – see you there!

#MeetYourCandidates #TitahiBay #PoriruaElections2025 #PariruaMāoriWard #OnepotoWard
label: Government & Politics

description:
Espress-Hoes PT 2, Addicted to that me-espresso? Or are you just straight up talking nonsense. Join some of Wellington's shortest and sweetest drag performers in celebrating the release of Sabrina Carpenters newest album, Man's Best Friend. Whether you're a Carpenter, or just love beautiful blonde women, you are sure to enjoy a night of Sabrina-fied performances. So please please please, make sure to get you tickets before the sell out. Wear something cute, maybe a feather or two, and enjoy a night of fast times.

Featuring:
Pita Pocket
Blink Bogan
Maria Richman
Ness Quick

Doors open at 9:30 for a show starting at 10:00
No Stag Do's

description:
Crazy Ladies, Pamela Browne has organised a 25 year reunion for her four best friends from High School. From the moment Kay, now a chocoholic gun-toting funeral director arrives, the wheels begin falling off Pamela’s meticulously planned weekend.
Added to the mix is Sandy, who is now a Nun, Dianne, married the school nerd and is a mother of eight sons, Rachel, a runaway teenager on a mission to dig up some dirt on her mother, and Shaun, the greasy motel janitor, who ends up in a compromising position in the ladies’ wardrobe.
Guns, gags, cuffs, crucifixes, sleaze-balls , slander, pizza and pinot – everything you need for a great weekend reunion!
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Meet the author Janus Lucky, Meet the author Janus Lucky
If you enjoy edgy murder mysteries, this event is for you. The local author, Janus Lucky, will be at the War Memorial Library on Wednesday, 10th September, from 6 pm to 7:30 pm, reading excerpts from his murder mystery, The Birthmark Murders – Death is a Cabaret, Old Chum, and his upcoming coming-of-age story, Handful – The Boy at the End of the Rainbow. He will answer questions and even sign books if you're interested in buying one. The library also kindly offers some refreshments for attendees. You are very welcome.

Also check out other Arts events in Lower Hutt, Literary Art events in Lower Hutt.

description:
There Once Was a Boy..., Written and performed by Robert Lloyd

A boy is stranded at sea. A storm bears down on him, and, in what he believes are his final moments, he ponders his life and the choice he made to come here—to explore what’s over the curious horizon—and wonders: Was this a mistake? There once was a boy… is a fun, poetic performance for tweens, teens and their whānau. This live “pop-up book” explores curiosity, courage, and finding light in the darkness.

Recommended for audiences aged 10+. Contains flashing lights.

This show was created as part of the TAHI Festival 2025 Creative Residency programme, in partnership with Creative NZ, PAYPA and Toi Poneke Arts Centre.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Soup & Solidarity, Join us for a relaxed, creative evening as craft solidarity books for both Ramallah (our sister city) and Gaza, to show our allyship from here in Aotearoa.
We’ll provide scrapbooking materials, but we encourage you to bring your own treasures. Items like stencils, photos, poems, and Aotearoa-inspired artwork are all welcome.
Join us to highlight shared culture through art, music, hopeful messages, and reflections on solidarity here at home.
This drop-in session continues the spirit of our Nakba Day gathering - connecting, reflecting, and expressing care. Expect warmth, soup, bread, tea, incense, beautiful music, and space to stay until late. This event is open to all ages.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
Cinderella, Glass slippers, royal balls, and a Fairy Godmother who's still on her learner's licence, what could possibly go wrong?

Prince Percy is hopelessly charming (emphasis on hopeless), the Queen is busy approving new royal wave techniques (it's very hard work being a Queen!), the step-sisters are causing chaos in couture, and Buttons has big dreams!

Will true friendship win over fairy-tale romance? Will Buttons dreams come true? Packed with music, mischief, and magical mayhem, this is Cinderella with a twist, a tune, and a whole lot of sparkle!

--
For accessibility seating information and wheelchair space bookings please email kiaora@teraukura.nz or call 04)9740535 during office hours.
label: Arts & Theatre
